summary: DHH considers buying an Apple iBook as his first laptop for school, surprising himself given his history of criticizing Mac users. He lists the iBook's strengths including price, networking, design, and the maturing OS X. He plans to test an older iBook from work before committing, while acknowledging the emotional pull of Apple's design.
key_points: DHH evaluates laptops from Sony, Dell, and Apple, with the iBook as a surprising contender
OS X maturing into a usable operating system is a key factor in considering Apple
He frames the switch as an opportunity to move beyond a Microsoft-only worldview
Despite enthusiasm, he plans a pragmatic trial before committing to the purchase
money_quotes: That's right, I'm actually considering buying Apple. Me who have been slamming fruitty people for ages. Go figure.
All that and of course the chance to try something else for a change.
Put down the axe. Seeing beyond Microsoft.
No garanties that I'll find her sexy enough, but she'll sure get a real chance (for the first time ever).
Must keep head straight. Think rational, not get seduced by that powder-snow white beuty.
key_insight: Even a self-described Apple skeptic can be won over when a platform matures enough to offer genuine advantages, foreshadowing DHH's eventual full embrace of the Mac ecosystem.
